Direct NPR-hosted NASA Terra/Worldview image shows fires burning in the United Arab Emirates during March 16 Gulf attacks

title: Iraq militia strikes US target in Baghdad
detail: Iran-allied militia launches two strikes on US target in Baghdad. Iraq says both intercepted. Hezbollah also strikes southern Lebanon.
dl: MINOR — Intercepted proxy attacks

title: IDF kills Basij paramilitary chief Gholamreza Soleimani
detail: Israeli strike kills head of IRGC Basij force Gholamreza Soleimani, his deputy, and other top officials at a tent camp set up after prior HQ strikes. Basij responsible for suppressing Iranian civilian protests.
dl: DESTROYED — Basij leadership eliminated

title: Israel targets Ali Larijani in Tehran airstrike
detail: Overnight airstrike targets Ali Larijani, secretary of Iran's National Security Council and key figure since Khamenei's death. Fate unclear. PIJ leader Akram al-Ajouri also targeted in separate strike on Tehran safe house.
dl: STRUCK — Result unclear, leadership targeted

title: IDF strikes military sites in Tehran, Shiraz, Tabriz
detail: Dozens of IAF jets drop bombs on security HQs, Intelligence Ministry, Basij facilities, drone/missile storage in Tehran. Ballistic missile storage hit in Shiraz. Air defense systems destroyed in Tabriz to expand air superiority.
dl: HEAVY — Multi-city military infrastructure struck

satelliteImages.description: Before-and-after satellite imagery showing damage to aircraft on tarmac at Shiraz air base, with scorch marks and debris around parking areas

title: Israel launches ground operation in southern Lebanon
detail: 91st Division begins limited, targeted ground operations against Hezbollah strongholds in southern Lebanon. IDF says 400+ Hezbollah fighters killed since March 2. Displacement orders issued south of Litani River.
dl: ACTIVE — Ground invasion launched

title: Israeli airstrikes hit three Beirut neighbourhoods
detail: Israeli air strikes rain down on three neighbourhoods in Beirut, continuing escalation against Hezbollah in Lebanon alongside the ground operation.
dl: HEAVY — Multiple urban areas struck

title: Massive drone/rocket barrage on US Embassy in Baghdad
detail: Most intense assault on US Embassy in Baghdad since war began. At least 5 drones and rockets used. Iraqi security sources describe it as heaviest attack yet. Two killed in separate strike on house with Iranian advisers.
dl: MODERATE — Embassy targeted, advisers killed

title: Missile debris kills 1 in Abu Dhabi; explosions in Dubai, Doha
detail: Falling shrapnel from intercepted missile kills Pakistani national in Abu Dhabi's Bani Yas area. Explosions echo across Dubai and Doha. Tanker struck 23nm east of Fujairah. Fire in Fujairah Oil Industry Zone from drone attack.
dl: MODERATE — Gulf capitals under fire

title: Israeli strikes on Iran's South Pars gas field
detail: Israel struck Iranian facilities at South Pars, the world's largest natural gas deposit shared with Qatar. Gas tanks and parts of a refinery hit; workers evacuated. Fire brought under control. Oil prices surged ~5% to above $108/bbl. Major escalation targeting energy infrastructure for the first time.
dl: HEAVY — Energy infrastructure struck

satelliteImages.description: High-resolution Airbus imagery shows medium to severe damage, burn scars, damaged production lines, and firefighting-water stains at South Pars refinery units after the March 18 Israeli strike

title: Israel kills intelligence minister Esmail Khatib in Tehran
detail: Israeli strike killed Iran's intelligence minister Esmail Khatib in Tehran, a day after killing security chief Ali Larijani and Basij commander Gholamreza Soleimani. Defence Minister Katz: 'no one in Iran has immunity and everyone is in the crosshairs.'
dl: MODERATE — Intelligence chief assassinated

title: Iranian missile kills 2 in Ramat Gan, Israel
detail: Rocket attack heavily damaged apartment building in Ramat Gan near Tel Aviv. Two people killed by severe shrapnel injuries. Israeli and US flags hung at impact site.
dl: MODERATE — Residential area struck

title: Iran fires missiles at Qatar's Ras Laffan gas facility
detail: QatarEnergy reported 'extensive damage' after Iranian missiles struck Ras Laffan Industrial City, one of the world's largest LNG hubs. Qatar expelled two senior Iranian diplomats and rebuked both Israel and Iran. Iran had warned of retaliation after South Pars strike.
dl: HEAVY — Major LNG hub damaged

satelliteImages.description: High-resolution Airbus imagery shows damage to Ras Laffan LNG South production lines 4 and 6 and the Pearl GTL production line 2 after Iranian strikes

title: Saudi Arabia intercepts 4 ballistic missiles toward Riyadh
detail: Saudi Arabia intercepted and destroyed four ballistic missiles launched at Riyadh and thwarted a drone attack on an eastern gas facility. Phone alerts sent to Riyadh residents for the first time. Saudi Arabia: 'the little trust that remained in Iran has been completely shattered.'
dl: MINOR — Missiles intercepted

title: Israeli strikes destroy buildings in central Beirut
detail: Israeli military hit central Beirut destroying apartment buildings in some of the most intense airstrikes on the Lebanese capital in decades. Zuqaq al-Blat district heavily impacted.
dl: HEAVY — Central Beirut devastated

title: Israeli strikes kill 4 in Bekaa Valley, Lebanon
detail: At least four people killed in Israeli attack targeting four houses in the town of Sahmar in Lebanon's Bekaa Valley.
dl: MODERATE — Residential targets struck

title: Iran fires cluster munitions at Israel; 1 killed in Adanim
detail: Iran launched cluster missiles at Israel, which are harder to intercept. After midnight, a foreign national died in Adanim in central Israel. Police detonated unexploded cluster bomb ordnance in northern Israel. Death toll in Israel reaches at least 15.
dl: MODERATE — Cluster munitions used

title: Iranian missile kills 3 Palestinians in West Bank
detail: Iranian missile attack killed at least 3 and injured several in the Israeli-occupied West Bank — the first Iranian strike to kill Palestinians in the West Bank since the war began.
dl: MODERATE — First Palestinian deaths from Iranian fire

title: Strike on Bushehr nuclear plant (no damage)
detail: IAEA reported Iran informed them of a strike on its only operational nuclear power plant at Bushehr. The UN nuclear watchdog confirmed it caused no damage.
dl: MINOR — No damage reported by IAEA

satelliteImages.description: High-resolution Airbus imagery confirmed a projectile impact crater about 350 meters from the Bushehr reactor, with no damage to the reactor itself.

title: Iran projectile strikes near Australian military HQ in UAE
detail: An Iranian projectile struck near Australia's military headquarters for the Middle East in the UAE. PM Albanese confirmed the incident. Warning sirens also sounded across Bahrain.
dl: MINOR — Near-miss on allied base

title: Five Iranian missile salvos target Jerusalem and northern Israel
detail: Iran launched five ballistic missile salvos targeting Jerusalem and northern Israel within one hour. Sirens sounded across northern Israel and Jerusalem. Cluster munitions struck Haifa, with burning fragments crashing into the city. A missile fragment lodged in the Golan Heights.
dl: MODERATE — Haifa refinery hit, cluster munitions used

title: Iranian strikes hit Ras Laffan LNG hub in Qatar
detail: Two waves of Iranian missiles caused 'extensive damage' at Qatar's Ras Laffan Industrial City, which processes ~20% of global LNG. QatarEnergy said damage will take 3-5 years to repair and wiped out 17% of Qatar's LNG capacity. Trump threatened to 'blow up the entirety of the South Pars Gas Field' if Iran attacked Qatar again.
dl: CRITICAL — 17% of Qatar LNG capacity destroyed

title: Iranian strike on Saudi Riyadh refineries
detail: Saudi Arabia reported two refineries struck in its capital Riyadh. Saudi Arabia's main Red Sea port, used to divert exports around Iran's Hormuz closure, was also attacked.
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Two refineries struck

title: IDF strikes Bandar Anzali port on Caspian Sea
detail: IDF targeted 'dozens' of vessels at the 4th Artesh Naval District HQ at Bandar Anzali, Gilan Province, including the Moudge-class frigate IRIS Deylaman. Also hit a command center, shipyard, customs building, and Shahid Tamjidi Offshore Industries (defense ministry subsidiary). Shut down a critical Iran-Russia supply line for wheat and military equipment via the Caspian Sea.
dl: CRITICAL — Iran-Russia Caspian supply line severed

satelliteImages.description: Substantial damage to Iranian naval and air infrastructure at 4th Artesh Naval District HQ at Bandar Anzali Port

title: IDF hits 130+ targets in western and central Iran
detail: IDF reported striking over 130 targets across western and central Iran over the past day, including IRGC missile sites near Borazjan (Bushehr Province) and the Shahroud Missile Facility launch pad in Semnan Province. Combined force has now destroyed ~85% of Iran's surface-to-air missiles.
dl: SIGNIFICANT — 85% of Iran's SAMs destroyed

title: Hezbollah long-range missile targets southern Israel
detail: Hezbollah launched its longest-range attack ever, triggering sirens in Ashkelon and areas near Gaza (~200km from Lebanon border). Likely used Fateh-110, Scud, or Zelzal-2 missiles.
dl: MODERATE — Record-range Hezbollah attack

title: Israel launches 'wave of strikes' on Tehran
detail: Israeli military announced targeted attacks on Iranian government infrastructure in Tehran in a 'wave of strikes' early Friday morning. IRGC spokesman Ali Mohammed Naini was killed in the strike. Iran's intelligence minister Esmail Khatib was also killed (funeral held same day).
dl: SIGNIFICANT — IRGC spokesman + intelligence minister killed

title: US intensifies Strait of Hormuz operations
detail: US warplanes and attack helicopters ramped up assaults against Iranian drones and naval vessels in the Strait of Hormuz in an effort to reopen the critical waterway. Iran has used mines, missiles and armed drones to effectively shut down shipping through the strait.
dl: SIGNIFICANT — US scrambling to reopen strait

title: Drone strike on Kuwait's Mina al-Ahmadi refinery
detail: A drone attack caused fires at Kuwait's Mina al-Ahmadi refinery for the second consecutive day, per Kuwait Petroleum Corporation.
dl: MODERATE — Second consecutive day of attacks

title: Iran launches drones/missiles at Gulf states
detail: Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, Bahrain and UAE reported intercepting drone and missile attacks from Iran. Falling shrapnel started a fire at a warehouse in Bahrain.
dl: MINOR — Mostly intercepted, shrapnel fires in Bahrain

title: US strikes Karaj missile assembly facility
detail: CENTCOM announced a targeted strike on a ballistic missile production and assembly facility in Karaj. The facility was used to assemble missiles threatening US civilians and regional countries.
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Missile production facility destroyed

satelliteImages.description: Before/after satellite imagery posted by CENTCOM shows damage to the Karaj Surface-to-Surface Missile Plant after a U.S. strike on the facility

title: IDF strikes 130+ targets across Iran
detail: IDF targeted more than 130 regime infrastructures including ballistic missile launchers, UAVs, and air defense systems across Tehran, Parchin, Kerman, Arak, and Bandar Lengeh. Additional strikes reported in Yazd, Borazjan, Hormozgan, and Ahvaz.
dl: SIGNIFICANT — 130+ military targets struck in broad wave

satelliteImages.description: Damaged buildings at Parchin military base outside Tehran, linked to ballistic missile program

title: US F-35 hit by anti-aircraft fire, emergency landing
detail: A US Air Force F-35 fighter jet was forced to make an emergency landing at an American base in the Middle East after being hit by anti-aircraft fire during a mission over Iran.
dl: MINOR — F-35 damaged but safely landed

title: Iran attacks US Embassy logistics base in Baghdad
detail: Iran claimed it targeted the US Embassy's logistics base in Baghdad three times in one day with rockets and drones. The embassy has faced multiple attacks since the war began.
dl: MINOR — Multiple attacks on embassy logistics

title: Israel strikes Tehran, Isfahan, and Karaj
detail: Israeli military launched fresh wave of strikes on Saturday targeting Tehran, Isfahan, and Karaj. Explosions reported across several parts of the Iranian capital and surrounding cities.
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Continued bombardment of multiple cities

title: Cluster missile hits Rishon Lezion, Israel
detail: Iran fired a ballistic missile carrying a cluster bomb warhead at central Israel. Bomblets struck several sites in Rishon Lezion including an empty daycare. No injuries reported.
dl: MINOR — Cluster munitions, property damage only

title: Iran fires ballistic missiles at Diego Garcia
detail: Iran fired two intermediate-range ballistic missiles at Diego Garcia, a joint US-UK military base in the Indian Ocean (~3,800 km away). Neither missile struck the base. Israel confirmed Iran used a two-stage ICBM with 4,000+ km range — a capability not previously revealed. Iran later denied the attack, calling it an "Israeli false flag." UK Foreign Secretary condemned the "reckless threats." Analysts noted this puts Europe within Iranian missile range.
dl: MISS — Both missiles failed to hit base

title: IDF strikes Hezbollah command centers in Beirut
detail: IAF struck Hezbollah command centers in Beirut overnight after issuing evacuation warnings for southern suburbs. Givati Brigade troops also killed several Hezbollah operatives in south Lebanon firefight.
dl: MODERATE — Command centers hit, operatives killed

title: US lifts Iran oil sanctions temporarily
detail: US paused sanctions on Iranian oil stranded on tankers at sea until April 19, seeking to curb soaring energy prices. Brent crude peaked above $116/barrel. Indian and Asian refiners plan to resume buying Iranian oil.
dl: DIPLOMATIC — Temporary sanctions relief on Iranian oil

title: Natanz nuclear enrichment facility struck again
detail: Iran's main nuclear enrichment site at Natanz was struck for the third time in the conflict. Israel denied responsibility. IAEA said no leakage reported. Mizan (Iranian judiciary media) confirmed no nuclear material leaked. Russia warned of "real risk of catastrophic disaster."
dl: HEAVY — Third strike on enrichment site, no leakage reported

title: Iranian missiles strike Dimona, near nuclear research center
detail: Iranian ballistic missiles struck the town of Dimona (~20 km from Israel's nuclear research center). Israeli air defenses launched interceptors but failed to hit the missiles. Over 40 people injured. First time Iranian missiles penetrated air defenses in the Dimona area.
dl: MODERATE — Ballistic missile direct hit, interceptors failed

title: Iranian missiles strike Arad, 59 injured
detail: Iranian ballistic missiles with warheads weighing hundreds of kilograms struck Arad (~35 km from Israel's nuclear research center). Interceptors launched but failed. Widespread damage across at least 10 apartment buildings, 3 in danger of collapsing. At least 59 injured.
dl: HEAVY — Multiple buildings damaged, 3 near collapse

title: Iranian drones strike Kuwait's Mina al-Ahmadi refinery
detail: Two waves of Iranian drones hit Kuwait's Mina al-Ahmadi refinery, one of the Middle East's largest (730,000 bpd capacity), sparking a fire. Kuwait MOD confirmed it is actively dealing with hostile missile and drone attacks.
dl: HEAVY — Fire at major refinery (730k bpd capacity)

title: Saudi Arabia intercepts 47 Iranian drones
detail: Saudi MOD reported intercepting and destroying 47 drones including a concentrated barrage of 38 drones within 3 hours over its eastern region. Bahrain reported destroying 143 missiles and 242 drones total since Feb 28.
dl: INTERCEPTED — 47 drones shot down

title: Trump threatens to destroy Iran's power plants — 48-hour ultimatum on Hormuz
detail: Trump posted on Truth Social giving Iran 48 hours to fully reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face destruction of power plants "STARTING WITH THE BIGGEST ONE FIRST." Iran warned any strike on energy facilities would prompt attacks on US and Israeli energy assets. Comes one day after Trump said he was considering "winding down" the operation.
dl: DIPLOMATIC — 48-hour ultimatum to reopen Hormuz

title: Iran claims Hormuz open to non-enemy ships
detail: Iran's representative to the UN IMO, Ali Mousavi, said the Strait of Hormuz is open to all vessels except ships of "enemy" countries. Called US-Israeli aggression the "root" cause of closure. Said diplomacy remains Iran's priority but cessation of aggression is essential.
dl: DIPLOMATIC — Iran says strait open except for enemy ships

title: 20+ nations sign joint statement demanding Hormuz reopening
detail: Germany, UAE, Bahrain, UK, France, Japan, South Korea, Australia and over 20 allies signed a joint statement to contribute to efforts to secure shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, condemning Iran's attacks on commercial vessels and oil/gas facilities.
dl: DIPLOMATIC — Joint demand to reopen Hormuz

title: Iranian missiles strike Dimona and Arad near Israeli nuclear site
detail: Two Iranian ballistic missiles hit the cities of Dimona and Arad in southern Israel, just 8 miles from Israel's main nuclear facility. More than 10 people seriously injured, dozens more hurt. Renewed concerns about whether Israel was holding back its most sophisticated air defenses. Israel cancelled in-person classes nationwide for March 22-23.
dl: MODERATE — Missiles hit near Israeli nuclear facility

title: Combined force strikes Khojir Aerospace Complex missile facilities
detail: IDF confirmed strikes on Defense Ministry weapons production and storage site at Khojir Aerospace Complex southeast of Tehran. Facility was final assembly site for Fath-360, Fateh-110, Zolfaghar, and Haj Qasem ballistic missiles. Explosions reported near Damavand town. CENTCOM published satellite imagery showing extensive damage.
dl: HEAVY — Missile assembly facility destroyed

satelliteImages.description: CENTCOM published satellite imagery showing extensive damage at Khojir Aerospace Complex missile assembly facility
Washington Post analysis: at least 88 structures destroyed at Khojir Military Complex, a key missile R&D and manufacturing facility

title: Strikes on Artesh base and IRGC facility near Karaj
detail: IDF struck an Artesh base used for personnel training and missile storage in southwest Tehran, plus an IRGC Aerospace Force weapons production facility west of Tehran. Multiple explosions reported in Karaj, Alborz Province.
dl: HEAVY — Military training and production facilities hit

title: 8th Tactical Air Base in Isfahan struck again
detail: Fire reported at 8th Tactical Air Base in Isfahan Province after renewed combined force strikes. Base previously hit on March 7 and 8. Satellite imagery also confirmed two craters on Bushehr Airport runway from strikes between March 14-22.
dl: HEAVY — Air base struck for third time

satelliteImages.description: Destroyed buildings at Isfahan airport area, later struck again
Direct Vantor image shows approximately a dozen aircraft destroyed at Khatami/8th Tactical Air Base near Isfahan after March strikes

title: Underground missile bases struck in Fars and Hormozgan
detail: Combined force struck exterior of underground missile base between Gerash and Lar in Fars Province (hit March 16 and 22). Also confirmed strikes on missile launchers and tunnel entrances at underground bases in Hajjiabad (Hormozgan) and Darab (Fars) from March 20.
dl: MODERATE — Tunnel entrances and launchers damaged

satelliteImages.description: WashPost analysis: 15 facilities destroyed and 2 tunnel entrances struck at Khorgu Missile Base, Hormozgan Province

title: Israel orders demolition of bridges over Litani River in Lebanon
detail: Defense Minister Israel Katz ordered military to step up demolition of bridges and houses in Lebanon. Lebanese President Joseph Aoun accused Israel of "suspicious plans" to establish a buffer zone in the south. Israel's military chief said campaign against Hezbollah had "only just begun."
dl: MODERATE — Bridge demolitions, possible occupation prep

satelliteImages.description: Before/after showing Khardali Bridge over Litani River destroyed; at least 7 bridges struck by Israeli forces

title: IRGC threatens to hit Gulf power plants and desalination facilities
detail: Iran's Revolutionary Guard said if US targets Iranian energy facilities, it will respond by hitting power plants supplying electricity to US bases, plus economic and industrial infrastructure with American shareholders. "Do not doubt that we will do this." Iran dismissed Trump's 48-hour Hormuz ultimatum and threatened to consider power plants and water facilities "legitimate targets."
dl: DIPLOMATIC — IRGC threatens regional energy infrastructure retaliation

title: Israel launches wide-scale strikes on Tehran — power outages across capital
detail: Israel announced "wide-scale wave of strikes" targeting Tehran infrastructure early Monday. Power went out across large parts of the capital with sustained strikes hitting eastern, western, and northern regions. Blackouts affected neighborhoods across the city of 10 million. Came as Trump's 48-hour Hormuz ultimatum neared expiry.
dl: HEAVY — Power grid struck, citywide blackouts

title: Iran threatens to mine entire Persian Gulf if coasts attacked
detail: Iran's Defence Council warned that any attack on Iran's southern coast or islands would trigger mine-laying across all Gulf routes with "various types of sea mines, including floating mines released from the coast." Said entire Gulf would be in situation similar to Strait of Hormuz "for a long time." US reportedly considering plans to occupy or blockade Kharg Island, Iran's main oil export hub.
dl: DIPLOMATIC — Iran threatens to mine entire Gulf

title: Asian stock markets tumble; oil surges past 13/barrel
detail: Asian markets fell sharply on escalation fears: South Korea's Kospi dropped 6.5%, Japan/China/Hong Kong fell ~3.5%. Brent crude rose above 13/barrel; US benchmark jumped above 00. Global economic impact widening — UN warned tens of millions more could face hunger. War has displaced 3.2 million in Iran and 1 million in Lebanon.
dl: ECONOMIC — Markets crash, oil spikes on escalation

title: Trump postpones power plant strikes, gives Iran 5-day window
detail: Trump abruptly shelved his 48-hour Hormuz ultimatum and announced "very good and productive conversations" with Iran. Gave a 5-day period for diplomacy. "We'll see how that goes, and if it goes well, we're going to end up with settling this. Otherwise, we'll keep bombing our little hearts out." Pakistan joining mediation efforts; in-person meeting may be held in Islamabad.
dl: DIPLOMATIC — Trump delays energy strikes, signals talks

title: CENTCOM: Over 9,000 strikes, 140+ Iranian naval vessels destroyed
detail: U.S. Central Command released operational summary: more than 9,000 Iranian targets struck, over 140 Iranian naval vessels damaged or destroyed, and upward of 9,000 combat flights flown since Operation Epic Fury began Feb 28. Described as largest naval defeat since WWII.
dl: MILESTONE — 9,000+ targets struck in 3.5 weeks

title: IDF strikes IRGC intelligence HQs and weapons storage in Tehran
detail: Israeli Air Force struck two IRGC intelligence headquarters, an Iranian Intelligence Ministry HQ, weapon storage sites, and air defense systems in Tehran. Netanyahu confirmed he spoke with Trump and that strikes in Iran and Lebanon will continue. Said Israel killed two additional nuclear scientists in recent days.
dl: HEAVY — Multiple intel HQs and weapons depots struck

title: IDF strikes 50+ targets overnight across northern and central Iran
detail: Overnight wave of Israeli airstrikes hit more than 50 targets in northern and central Iran, including sites used to store and launch ballistic missiles. Continued systematic campaign against Iran's missile infrastructure.
dl: HEAVY — 50+ missile sites and launch facilities struck

title: Isfahan and Khorramshahr energy infrastructure struck
detail: Airstrikes hit the gas administration building and a gas pressure reduction station on Kaveh Street in Isfahan, damaging facilities and nearby homes. A gas pipeline for the Khorramshahr power plant was also targeted. Governor said projectile struck area outside the pipeline station with no casualties.
dl: MODERATE — Energy infrastructure hit in Isfahan and Khorramshahr

title: Iranian ballistic missile hits Tel Aviv — 3 buildings damaged
detail: Iranian warhead impacted a street in central Tel Aviv, causing significant damage to three buildings. Four people lightly injured, none hospitalized. Home Front Command assessed it was a standalone warhead (not cluster munition) but smaller than previous conventional warheads. At least 7 Iranian missile launches since midnight targeting southern and central Israel.
dl: MODERATE — Warhead impact in central Tel Aviv, 4 light injuries

title: Hezbollah fires 30 rockets at Haifa Bay area
detail: Hezbollah launched a barrage of approximately 30 rockets from Lebanon targeting the Haifa Bay area. Most rockets intercepted by air defenses. Sirens sounded in Acre and Krayot suburbs. No injuries reported.
dl: LIGHT — 30 rockets mostly intercepted, no injuries

title: Israel strikes Beirut southern suburbs — first in days
detail: Series of strikes hit Beirut's Dahiyeh southern suburbs early Tuesday, the first attack on the Hezbollah stronghold in several days. Israeli military said it captured two Hezbollah members in southern Lebanon. IDF spokesperson said "the battle against Hezbollah has only just begun." Lebanon health ministry reports 1,039 killed since conflict started.
dl: HEAVY — Strikes resume on Hezbollah stronghold

title: Iranian cluster munitions hit Bnei Brak — 9 injured
detail: Iranian ballistic missile cluster munitions impacted the city of Bnei Brak near Tel Aviv, injuring 9 people. First responders treated casualties at the scene. The Home Front Command assessed the strike involved cluster bomb submunitions.
dl: MODERATE — Cluster munitions injure 9 in Bnei Brak

title: Bushehr nuclear plant hit again — IAEA says no damage
detail: A projectile hit the premises of the Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant for the second time during the conflict. Iran informed the IAEA there was no damage to the facility or injuries to staff. IAEA Director-General Grossi reiterated calls for maximum restraint to avoid nuclear safety risks.
dl: LIGHT — Projectile hits plant premises, no damage reported

title: IDF strikes two naval cruise missile production sites in Tehran
detail: IAF fighter jets struck two key facilities used by the Iranian regime to develop and manufacture long-range naval cruise missiles capable of targeting ships and land targets. Massive blasts heard in northern Tehran and the city centre.
dl: HEAVY — Two cruise missile production sites destroyed

title: IRGC fires missiles at Israel, Kuwait, Jordan and Bahrain
detail: Iran's Revolutionary Guards announced they had fired precision-guided liquid- and solid-fuel missiles and attack drones at Israel and US military bases in Kuwait, Jordan, and Bahrain. Sirens sounded in Jerusalem; explosions heard over Jerusalem and Jericho. Three wounded in southern Israel, four minor injuries in Tel Aviv.
dl: MODERATE — Multi-target missile salvo hits Israel and Gulf bases

satelliteImages.source: Sentinel-2 / Soar analysis via Yedioth Ahronoth/Middle East Monitor
satelliteImages.description: Sentinel-2 imagery analysis reported visible damage at a defensive position inside Nevatim Airbase on March 25, during the multi-target IRGC missile/drone salvo against Israel and regional bases

title: Iranian drones hit Kuwait International Airport — fuel tank fire
detail: Iranian drones struck Kuwait International Airport and multiple other locations, hitting a fuel tank and triggering a large fire with thick plumes of smoke. Kuwaiti air defences responded to hostile missile and drone attacks.
dl: HEAVY — Fuel tank fire at Kuwait airport after drone strike

title: Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon — 9 killed, dozens injured
detail: Israeli attacks across southern Lebanon killed 9 and injured dozens. Nabatiyeh: 3 killed, 18 injured. Sidon: 6 killed, 5 wounded. Tyre: 29 injured. IDF said it captured two Hezbollah members in southern Lebanon. Lebanon health ministry total since conflict: 1,039 killed.
dl: HEAVY — 9 killed across Nabatiyeh, Sidon, Tyre

title: Iran blocks vessel in Strait of Hormuz bound for Karachi
detail: Iran halted a ship heading to Karachi that had not obtained required clearance to transit the Strait of Hormuz. Iran's mission in New York announced 'non-hostile vessels' may benefit from safe passage if they comply with Iranian security regulations and do not support aggression against Iran.
dl: BLOCKADE — Vessel seized, conditional passage announced

title: Lebanon expels Iran's ambassador — declares persona non grata
detail: Lebanon's Foreign Ministry declared Iran's ambassador to Beirut persona non grata and ordered him to leave by end of week. Marks the clearest sign of deteriorating Lebanese-Iranian relations amid the conflict.
dl: DIPLOMATIC — Lebanon expels Iranian ambassador

title: 82nd Airborne deploying ~1,000 troops to Middle East
detail: Pentagon deploying approximately 1,000 troops from the 82nd Airborne Division's 1st Brigade Combat Team to the Middle East, including division commander Maj Gen Brandon Tegtmeier and staff. The unit is the Army's emergency response force.
dl: DEPLOYMENT — 82nd Airborne emergency deployment

title: US sends 15-point ceasefire plan to Iran via Pakistan
detail: The Trump administration sent a 15-point ceasefire proposal to Iran through Pakistani intermediaries. Pakistan PM Sharif offered to host talks. Iran's military publicly mocked the offer, stating 'Don't dress up your defeat as an agreement.' Reports suggest Iran may prefer talks with VP Vance over envoy Witkoff.
dl: DIPLOMATIC — 15-point ceasefire plan sent, Iran rejects

title: Saudi Arabia intercepts 32 drones and ballistic missile in Eastern Province
detail: Saudi Ministry of Defence reported intercepting at least 32 Iranian drones and a ballistic missile targeting the Eastern Province, home to major oil facilities including Ras Tanura, Ghawar and Abqaiq. Limited material damage, no casualties reported.
dl: INTERCEPTED — 32 drones + 1 ballistic missile shot down

title: Moroccan civilian killed in Iranian attack on Bahrain
detail: UAE reported that an Iranian attack in Bahrain killed a Moroccan civilian working alongside UAE armed forces. Part of broader IRGC retaliatory strikes on Gulf states hosting US bases.
dl: STRIKE — 1 civilian killed

title: Netanyahu announces 30km 'security zone' in Lebanon
detail: Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu declared Israel's military will seize control of an area 30km into Lebanon as a 'security zone' to press the fight against Hezbollah. Canada condemned the plan as violating Lebanese sovereignty.
dl: MILITARY — 30km buffer zone declared

title: Second US strike on Anbar paramilitary base in Iraq
detail: Two missiles fired from a fighter jet at the same Iraqi paramilitary base in Anbar province struck on March 24. The first strike was the deadliest in Iraq since the war began. Iraq summoned both US and Iranian diplomats.
dl: STRIKE — Second attack on paramilitary base

title: Iran rejects US 15-point ceasefire plan — 'maximalist and unreasonable'
detail: Iran confirmed receiving the US 15-point peace proposal but a senior diplomatic source dismissed it as 'extremely maximalist and unreasonable'. FM Araghchi said 'we do not intend to negotiate' and 'speaking of negotiations now is an admission of defeat'. Trump warned he would 'unleash hell' if Iran doesn't accept a deal.
dl: DIPLOMATIC — Iran rejects US ceasefire terms

title: Israel strikes residential area and infrastructure across Tehran
detail: IDF launched a wave of strikes targeting infrastructure across Tehran. Semi-official Iranian SNN News Agency reported strikes hit a residential area with rescuers searching rubble. Continues Day 27 of the war.
dl: STRIKE — Residential area hit, rescuers in rubble

title: Iran FM: Strait of Hormuz 'closed only to enemies'
detail: FM Araghchi clarified Iran's Hormuz policy on state TV: 'The Strait of Hormuz is not completely closed — it is closed only to enemies. There is no reason to allow the ships of our enemies and their allies to pass.' ~2,000 vessels and 20,000 seafarers remain stranded.
dl: BLOCKADE — Hormuz 'closed only to enemies'

title: Australia bans Iranian visitors for 6 months
detail: Australia banned visitors travelling on Iranian passports from entering for tourism or work for six months, citing risk they would refuse to return home due to the war. Case-by-case exceptions for parents of Australian citizens.
dl: DIPLOMATIC — Australia bans Iranian visitors

title: Israel and US launch extensive strikes on central Isfahan
detail: Israel announced extensive strikes on central Isfahan. Alongside US forces, Israel launched a wave of extensive strikes across Iran, targeting missile and drone production facilities, reportedly hitting two-thirds of capacities.
dl: STRIKE — Extensive attacks on Isfahan facilities

satelliteImages.description: Damage to main explosives production facility southwest of Isfahan City; previously struck in June 2025 war

title: Saudi Arabia intercepts drones targeting Eastern Province
detail: Saudi Arabia’s Defence Ministry announced the interception and destruction of at least two dozen drones targeting the Eastern Province, home to the majority of the kingdom’s oil facilities.
dl: INTERCEPTION — Drones targeting oil facilities destroyed

title: Wave of Iranian missiles target Israel
detail: Israel reported a wave of missiles from Iran heading towards central and northern Israel, marking the second salvo in less than 30 minutes, alongside volleys of rockets from Hezbollah into the Western Galilee region.
dl: STRIKE — Iranian missiles and Hezbollah rockets fired

title: Iran plans tolls for Strait of Hormuz
detail: The Iranian parliament is preparing a draft law that would mandate the collection of tolls and duties from ships and tankers transiting the Strait of Hormuz, treating the critical waterway like a standard transit corridor.
dl: ECONOMIC — Tolls planned for Strait of Hormuz

title: Kuwait uncovers Hezbollah assassination plot
detail: Kuwait’s Interior Ministry announced the arrest of six individuals allegedly linked to Hezbollah, accused of planning assassinations within the Gulf state.
dl: SECURITY — Hezbollah assassination plot thwarted

title: Israel strikes Iran nuclear facilities — Arak heavy water plant and Ardakan uranium plant
detail: Israel struck the Shahid Khondab Heavy Water Complex in Arak and the Ardakan yellowcake production plant in Yazd Province. Iran's Atomic Energy Organization confirmed the strikes but said no casualties or radiation release occurred. Israel described the Yazd plant as key to Iran's enrichment program. Israel had threatened to 'escalate and expand' its campaign hours earlier.
dl: STRIKE — Nuclear facilities hit, no radiation release

title: Ardakan yellowcake uranium processing plant struck
detail: US-Israeli strikes hit the Ardakan yellowcake production plant in Yazd Province. Yellowcake is concentrated uranium used in enrichment. The Israeli military said the strike was a 'major blow' to Iran's nuclear program. No radiation release reported.
dl: STRIKE — Yellowcake plant hit, 'major blow' to nuclear program

title: US-Israeli strikes damage two major Iranian steel plants
detail: US-Israeli air strikes damaged two major steel plants in Iran, according to Iranian media reports. Part of ongoing campaign targeting industrial infrastructure.
dl: STRIKE — Two major steel plants damaged

title: Khondab heavy water plant struck — IAEA confirms hit
detail: The IAEA confirmed a heavy water production plant at Khondab was hit. 'No radiation risk seen as installation contains no declared nuclear material,' the UN nuclear watchdog said. Iran also reported the Khuzestan steel production factory using radioactive sources was struck; no off-site radiation release.
dl: STRIKE — IAEA confirms hit, no radiation risk

title: Iran strikes Prince Sultan Air Base — 10+ US troops wounded, planes damaged
detail: Iran fired 6 ballistic missiles and 29 drones at Saudi Arabia's Prince Sultan Air Base, wounding at least 10-15 US service members (2-5 seriously) and damaging several US refueling aircraft. Total US wounded since Feb 28 now exceeds 303. The base is 96km from Riyadh.
tn: Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia
dl: STRIKE — 10+ US troops wounded, refueling aircraft damaged

satelliteImages.description: 43 aircraft on tarmac at Prince Sultan Air Base including 13 KC-135 tankers and 6 E-3 AWACS planes before Iranian strike

title: Iran fires missiles and drones at UAE — debris kills 2 in Abu Dhabi
detail: Iran fired missiles and drones at the UAE. Debris from an intercepted projectile in Abu Dhabi killed two people (from India and Pakistan) and injured three. At least one injured was also from India.
dl: STRIKE — Intercepted debris kills 2 civilians

title: Kuwait's Shuwaikh port damaged by Iranian drone attack
detail: Kuwait's main commercial port at Shuwaikh was targeted by enemy drones at dawn. The Kuwait port authority reported material damage but no human casualties.
dl: STRIKE — Main commercial port damaged, no casualties

title: US strikes Habbaniyah base in Iraq — 5-7 Iraqi soldiers killed
detail: The US struck Habbaniyah base in Iraq's Anbar province, killing between 5 and 7 Iraqi soldiers and wounding 23.
dl: STRIKE — 5-7 Iraqi soldiers killed, 23 wounded

title: Israel renews strikes on Beirut's southern suburbs
detail: Israel's military renewed attacks on Beirut's southern suburbs, targeting Hezbollah infrastructure. Hezbollah said it had clashed directly with Israeli forces in south Lebanon. Two Israeli soldiers killed in south Lebanon. Death toll in Lebanon from Israeli attacks reached 1,116.
dl: STRIKE — Hezbollah infrastructure targeted, clashes in south

title: Trump delays energy strikes 10 days — extends Hormuz deadline to April 6
detail: Trump paused planned attacks on Iranian energy plants until April 6 at 8pm ET (00:00 GMT April 7), claiming peace talks are 'going very well'. Iran called the US proposal 'one-sided and unfair' and presented five non-negotiable demands. Rubio said the US expects the operation to conclude in 'weeks, not months'.
dl: DIPLOMATIC — Energy strikes delayed to April 6, talks disputed

title: Iran agrees to allow humanitarian aid through Strait of Hormuz
detail: Iran's UN ambassador Ali Bahreini said Tehran accepted a UN request to 'facilitate and expedite' humanitarian aid and agricultural shipments through the Strait of Hormuz. The UN announced a task force to address the war's ripple effects on aid delivery and fertilizer trade.
dl: DIPLOMATIC — Iran to allow humanitarian aid through Hormuz

title: G7 Foreign Ministers demand end to attacks on civilians
detail: G7 Foreign Ministers called for an immediate stop to attacks against civilians and civilian infrastructure in the Iran war. They underscored the importance of minimizing the impact on regional partners and mitigating global economic shocks to energy, fertilizer, and supply chains.
dl: DIPLOMATIC — G7 demands end to civilian attacks

title: Iran bans sports teams from traveling to 'hostile' countries
detail: Iran banned national sports teams from traveling to 'hostile' countries. The ban did not explicitly mention the FIFA World Cup in the US, Canada, and Mexico this summer, but singled out an upcoming soccer playoff game scheduled in Saudi Arabia in April.
dl: DIPLOMATIC — Sports teams banned from hostile nations

title: IRGC threatens hotels housing US soldiers will be targeted
detail: Iran's Revolutionary Guard said hotels housing US soldiers in the region will be targeted, escalating threats against US military presence.
dl: THREAT — IRGC warns hotels housing US troops will be hit

title: Iran fires missiles at Israel — 1 killed in Tel Aviv
detail: Iran launched missiles at Israel late Friday. Sirens sounded in Tel Aviv, Jerusalem, Beer Sheba, and near Israel's main nuclear research center. A 52-year-old man was killed in Tel Aviv. Hezbollah simultaneously fired rockets into Western Galilee.
dl: STRIKE — Iranian missile kills 1 in Tel Aviv

title: US confirms 303 troops wounded since war began
detail: US Central Command confirmed approximately 303 US service members have been wounded since Operation Epic Fury began on Feb 28. Of those, 273 have returned to duty. 10 remain seriously wounded. One soldier, Army Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington, 26, died days after being wounded in a March 1 attack.
dl: MILESTONE — 303 US troops wounded, 1 death confirmed

title: Houthis fire first missiles at Israel from Yemen — intercepted
detail: Yemen's Houthi rebels fired ballistic missiles at 'sensitive Israeli military sites' in southern Israel, their first attack since the war began. Israel's military said it intercepted a missile launched from Yemen. The Houthis had stayed out of the war until now. Their involvement raises concerns about renewed Red Sea shipping attacks.
dl: INTERCEPTED — First Houthi attack of the war

title: Iran strikes Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia — ~24 US troops wounded
detail: Iran launched missile and drone strikes at Saudi Arabia's Prince Sultan Air Base near Riyadh, wounding approximately 24 US service members (at least 2 seriously). Two military aircraft were also damaged. Iran released Chinese satellite photos showing burning aircraft. One of the most serious breaches of American defenses since the war began.
tn: Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia
dl: STRIKE — ~24 US troops wounded, aircraft damaged

satelliteImages.description: Satellite imagery showing three US KC-135 Stratotankers destroyed and burning at Prince Sultan Air Base after Iranian missile/drone strike

title: Israel strikes heavy water reactor in Iran
detail: Israel's military said it struck a heavy water reactor in Iran, which it described as part of Iran's nuclear enrichment program. The strike is part of ongoing efforts to degrade Iran's nuclear capabilities.
dl: STRUCK — Nuclear-related heavy water reactor hit

title: Heaviest day of strikes — 701 attacks in 24 hours across Iran
detail: HRANA documented 701 strikes over 24 hours on Saturday, among the highest for a single day since the war began. At least 24 civilians killed and 88 injured. Three-quarters of airstrikes hit Tehran. Israel said it 'completed a wide-scale wave of strikes targeting dozens of infrastructure across Tehran' including weapons storage, production sites, command centers, and air defense systems. Since the start, HRANA has recorded at least 1,551 civilian deaths including 236 children.
dl: MASSIVE — 701 strikes in one day, heaviest since war began

title: MIO, Parchin, and SADRA defense sites struck
detail: US-Israeli coalition struck Iranian defense industrial sites including Iran's Marine Industries Organization (MIO) and Parchin Military Complex in Tehran, and the Iran Marine Industrial Company (SADRA) in Bushehr Province. Steel factories across Iran also targeted.
dl: STRUCK — Major defense industrial sites hit

satelliteImages.description: WashPost analysis: 12 structures struck at Parchin Military Complex, including solid rocket propellant motor production facilities and Taleghan 2 site

satelliteImages.source: Vantor WorldView-Legion via GlobalSecurity/Drop Site News
satelliteImages.description: Direct Vantor image shows three penetration craters at Taleghan-2 inside the Parchin military complex

title: Iran University of Science and Technology struck in Tehran
detail: US-Israeli strikes hit the Iran University of Science and Technology in downtown Tehran, damaging research and educational buildings. The university has been linked to defense research. IRGC threatened retaliatory attacks on Israeli and American colleges in the Middle East.
tn: Iran University of Science and Technology, Tehran
dl: STRUCK — University hit, IRGC threatens college retaliation

title: Kuwait airport radar system damaged by Iranian drone
detail: Kuwait International Airport suffered significant damage to its radar system after an Iranian drone struck the facility. Kuwait's military confirmed the incident.
dl: DAMAGED — Airport radar system knocked out

title: Iran strikes aluminium plants in Bahrain and Abu Dhabi
detail: Aluminium Bahrain (Alba) was targeted in an Iranian attack, leaving two employees with minor injuries. Emirates Global Aluminium's Al Taweelah site in Abu Dhabi sustained significant damage from Iranian missiles and drones, injuring several employees. Separately, 5 people were injured in Abu Dhabi after a missile was intercepted near KEZAD industrial zone.
dl: STRUCK — Gulf aluminium industry targeted

title: 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit arrives in Middle East
detail: The Japan-based 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit, comprising approximately 2,500 sailors and Marines aboard the USS Tripoli amphibious assault group, arrived in the Middle East. The force is equipped for amphibious operations, raising speculation about possible ground assault scenarios.
dl: DEPLOYMENT — 2,500 Marines arrive for amphibious ops

title: Houthis launch second attack on Israel — drone intercepted over Eilat
detail: Following their first-ever missile attack on Day 28, the Houthis launched a second attack with drones and cruise missiles targeting southern Israel. Israeli air defense intercepted a Houthi drone over Eilat. Houthi spokesman Yahya Saree declared attacks would continue 'until the aggression ends.'
dl: INTERCEPTED — Second Houthi attack, drone over Eilat

title: Pakistan brokers Hormuz passage for 20 ships; Iran internet blackout hits 30 days
detail: Pakistan's deputy PM announced Iran agreed to let 20 vessels sailing under Pakistani flag through the Strait of Hormuz (2 per day). Meanwhile, Iran's internet blackout reached 30 days — connectivity at just 1% of normal levels per NetBlocks, cutting ~92 million people off from the outside world.
dl: DIPLOMACY — Pakistan ships to transit Hormuz; 30-day internet blackout

title: Bandar Khamir port bombed — at least 5 killed
detail: Iranian state media reported that the US-Israeli coalition bombarded the southern Iranian port of Bandar Khamir on Sunday, killing at least 5 people. No immediate comment from Israeli or American militaries.
dl: STRUCK — Port city bombed, 5 killed

title: Pakistan hosts 4-nation talks to de-escalate war
detail: Foreign ministers from Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Turkey, and Egypt convened in Islamabad for two days of talks to de-escalate the conflict. Iran's parliament speaker accused US of plotting ground attack despite diplomatic efforts. Trump's 15-point peace plan, delivered via Pakistan, was publicly rebuffed by Iran.
dl: DIPLOMACY — 4-nation talks in Pakistan; Iran rebuffs US peace plan

title: Israel launches new wave of strikes on Tehran — Day 30
detail: Israeli Air Force said it carried out another wave of strikes targeting infrastructure in Tehran and other areas on Sunday (Day 30). Iran retaliated with more drones and missiles at Israel and US Gulf allies. Saudi Arabia intercepted 10 drones in the morning hours. An Israeli soldier was killed in southern Lebanon.
dl: ONGOING — Day 30 strikes continue on Tehran

title: Al Araby TV headquarters struck in Tehran
detail: Israeli missile struck the Tehran headquarters of Al Araby TV, causing extensive damage and forcing suspension of live broadcasting. Footage showed broken windows and shattered glass throughout the building.
dl: STRUCK — Al Araby TV headquarters damaged, broadcasting halted

title: Iran strikes Kuwait power/desalination plant — Indian worker killed
detail: Iranian forces attacked a power and water desalination plant in Kuwait, killing an Indian national worker. Kuwait's defense ministry reported 14 ballistic missiles and 12 drones launched within its airspace in 24 hours, with 10 military personnel injured at a base and a private logistics warehouse damaged.
dl: STRUCK — Power plant hit, 1 killed, 10 military injured

title: UN peacekeeper (Indonesian) killed in southern Lebanon
detail: A projectile hit a UNIFIL position near a southern Lebanese village, killing an Indonesian peacekeeper and critically injuring another. UN Secretary General Guterres condemned the attack and noted several incidents jeopardizing peacekeeper safety in 48 hours. Indonesia condemned the killing.
dl: KILLED — Indonesian UN peacekeeper killed at UNIFIL post

title: Tabriz petrochemical facility struck
detail: US-Israeli airstrike targeted a petrochemical facility in Tabriz, northwestern Iran, around midnight. Iranian authorities in East Azerbaijan Province said the situation was brought under control with no dangerous substances released.
dl: STRUCK — Petrochemical plant hit, contained

satelliteImages.description: Damage visible at Tabriz Oil Refinery in East Azerbaijan Province after combined force strikes

title: Iran confirms IRGC Navy commander Tangsiri killed
detail: Iran confirmed that IRGC Navy Commander Admiral Alireza Tangsiri 'succumbed to severe injuries' from an Israeli airstrike in Bandar Abbas on March 26. Tangsiri led Iran's effort to close the Strait of Hormuz and oversaw IRGC Navy cruise missile testing and armed drone development. US CENTCOM had previously confirmed the kill.
dl: KILLED — IRGC Navy commander Tangsiri confirmed dead

title: US Special Operations forces arrive in Middle East
detail: Several hundred US Special Operations forces — including Army Rangers and Navy SEALs — arrived in the Middle East, joining 2,500 Marines (31st MEU), 2,500 sailors, and 2,000 Army 82nd Airborne paratroopers. Total US forces in region now exceed 50,000. Commandos could be deployed for Hormuz operations, Kharg Island seizure, or Isfahan uranium extraction.
dl: DEPLOYED — US SOF arrive; 50k+ troops now in region

title: Israel expands 'security zone' in southern Lebanon
detail: Netanyahu announced Israeli forces were 'expanding the security zone' in southern Lebanon to counter Hezbollah. Over 1,200 Lebanese killed since March 2. 3 Lebanese journalists (including Al Manar presenter Ali Choeib) killed in an Israeli airstrike. Lebanon's president warned the operations were seen as a prelude to full invasion.
dl: EXPANDING — Israel widens buffer zone; 1,200+ killed since March 2

title: Israel strikes Beirut's southern suburbs — Hezbollah targets
detail: Israeli military launched strikes on Hezbollah infrastructure in Beirut's southern suburbs (Dahieh) after issuing evacuation warnings. First strike in the area since Friday. AFPTV showed plumes of smoke rising. Area largely deserted since Israel began frequent attacks in early March.
dl: STRUCK — IDF hits Hezbollah infrastructure in Dahieh

title: Strikes damage Tehran and Alborz power grid — later restored
detail: US-Israeli strikes hit electrical infrastructure in Tehran and Alborz provinces overnight, cutting power to parts of the capital and surrounding areas. Iran's deputy Energy Minister confirmed damage but said the national grid remained stable and power was restored by Monday morning.
dl: STRUCK — Power grid damaged overnight, restored Monday

title: Trump says Iran agreed to release 20 more ships through Hormuz
detail: Trump announced Iran would allow 20 more oil cargo ships through the Strait of Hormuz starting Monday, calling it a 'sign of respect.' He claimed direct and indirect negotiations were underway and said he had achieved 'regime change' since original Iranian leaders were killed. Brent crude hit $116/barrel; oil up 56% since war began. Iran demands reparations, Hormuz sovereignty recognition, and sanctions relief as preconditions.
dl: DIPLOMACY — Trump claims Iran will release 20 ships; Brent at $116

title: IAEA confirms Khondab heavy-water plant destroyed and non-operational
detail: The International Atomic Energy Agency confirmed that Iran's heavy-water production plant at Khondab — attacked by Israel on March 27 — sustained severe damage and is no longer operational. The IAEA noted the Khondab heavy water research reactor contained no declared nuclear material.
dl: DESTROYED — IAEA confirms heavy-water plant non-operational

title: IRGC threatens to target US universities in Middle East
detail: Iran's Revolutionary Guards threatened to attack US universities in the Middle East after claiming US-Israeli strikes deliberately targeted two Iranian universities. IRGC set a noon Monday March 30 deadline for the US to condemn the university bombings, threatening to expand attacks to more institutions if conditions weren't met.
dl: THREAT — IRGC demands US condemn university strikes by noon Monday

title: Iranian drone strikes fully loaded Kuwaiti oil tanker off Dubai
detail: A Kuwaiti crude carrier fully loaded with two million barrels of oil was struck by an Iranian drone attack and set on fire early Tuesday while anchored at the Dubai port in the United Arab Emirates. The UAE authorities reported there was no oil leakage despite the fire. The attack came a day after US President Donald Trump renewed threats to target Iran's energy facilities if the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened.
dl: STRIKE — Kuwaiti tanker carrying 2M barrels of oil hit by drone and set on fire

title: US gasoline prices hit  a gallon as oil surge continues
detail: The national average for a gallon of gasoline in the US surpassed , as the Iran war and ongoing blockades in the Strait of Hormuz wreaked havoc on global energy supplies. Brent crude oil prices remained elevated above 15 per barrel following the attack on a Kuwaiti tanker off Dubai and continued threats against regional energy infrastructure.
dl: ECONOMY — US average gas prices hit /gallon amid energy supply crisis

title: Four Israeli soldiers killed in southern Lebanon combat
detail: The Israeli military reported that four personnel were killed and two others injured during combat operations against Hezbollah in southern Lebanon. Israeli troops have continued pushing deeper into Lebanese territory to dismantle militant infrastructure while the broader war with Iran unfolds.
dl: CASUALTIES — Four Israeli soldiers killed in combat with Hezbollah

title: IDF strikes air defense site in Mazandaran, northern Iran
detail: IDF expanded air dominance by striking an air defense site in Nowshahr, Mazandaran Province, near the Caspian Sea coast. IDF stated it has now destroyed more than 80% of Iran's air defense systems. Explosions also reported in Amol, Chalus, Babol, Nur, and Babolsar in Mazandaran.
dl: STRUCK — Air defense site destroyed; 80%+ of Iran SAMs now gone

satelliteImages.description: Destroyed air defense site in Nowshahr, Mazandaran Province near Caspian Sea coast

title: IDF strikes ~40 defense industrial sites in two days
detail: IDF struck approximately 40 defense industrial sites over past 48 hours including: IRGC Ballistic Missile Research Complex (near Khojir), Artesh Air Defense Research Organization (missile components), Parchin Military Complex (Shahid Sayyad Shirazi Industries), Saba Battery Factory (DIO subsidiary), Iranian Aviation Industries Organization (IAIO), long-range anti-aircraft missile assembly site in Ghazvin Province, and multiple facilities at Imam Hossein University (wind tunnels, chemistry center, physics center, weapons development center).
dl: MASSIVE — ~40 defense industrial sites struck in 48 hours

satelliteImages.description: WashPost analysis: 19 structures struck at Hakimiyeh Military Complex, a liquid propellant and missile launcher production facility in Tehran
Damage to Saba Battery warehouse in Pasargad Industrial Zone, Tehran; a DIO subsidiary producing missile components

title: Tabriz Oil Refinery and Abadan Refinery struck
detail: Combined force struck Tabriz Oil Refinery in East Azerbaijan Province and explosions reported near Abadan Oil Refinery in Khuzestan Province. Large explosion footage circulated. First confirmed strikes on oil refineries inside Iran. Neither CENTCOM nor IDF confirmed at time of reporting.
dl: STRUCK — Two major oil refineries hit

title: 2nd Tactical Airbase in Tabriz struck again
detail: Combined force struck Artesh Air Force 2nd Tactical Airbase in Tabriz, East Azerbaijan Province, with seven explosions reported. Base houses MiG-29 and F-5 aircraft from 21st, 22nd, and 23rd tactical fighter squadrons. Previously struck around March 3 and during June 2025 war.
dl: STRUCK — Airbase hit for third time in the war

satelliteImages.description: Damage at 2nd Tactical Airbase in Tabriz housing MiG-29 and F-5 aircraft; base struck for third time in the war

title: Two missile bases near Shiraz struck
detail: Combined force struck two missile bases near Shiraz, Fars Province, including the Shiraz South Missile Base — one of Iran's 25 primary MRBM launch bases and previously used to fire missiles at Israel. Ground-penetrating munitions targeted underground facilities.
dl: STRUCK — Underground missile launch facilities targeted

satelliteImages.description: Ground-penetrating munitions craters visible at Shiraz South Missile Base, one of Iran's 25 primary MRBM launch bases

title: Basij and police sites struck in Iranian Kurdistan
detail: IDF struck Basij bases in Dehgolan and Divandarreh, Kurdistan Province, and Law Enforcement Command buildings in Sanandaj and Saghez. These cities were previous hotspots of Iranian protest suppression in January 2026.
dl: STRUCK — Internal security sites in protest hotspots targeted

title: NATO intercepts Iranian ballistic missile over Turkish airspace — 4th time
detail: Turkish Defense Ministry announced NATO air defense systems intercepted an Iranian ballistic missile that entered Turkish airspace over the eastern Mediterranean Sea — the fourth such violation since the war began. Iran's defense minister called Turkish counterpart to discuss; Iran previously denied responsibility.
dl: INTERCEPTED — 4th Iranian missile in Turkish airspace

title: PMF fighters deploy to western Iran
detail: Anti-regime media and a KDP-affiliated journalist reported Popular Mobilization Forces (PMF) fighters from Iraq have deployed to Basij bases in Khorramshahr and Abadan, Khuzestan Province. Iran may be using PMF to strengthen control over protest hotspots as combined force struck Shalamcheh border crossing.
dl: DEPLOYMENT — Iraqi PMF fighters enter Iran to bolster Basij

title: Trump: 13,000 targets struck; 150 vessels destroyed; 3,000 targets remain
detail: Trump told Financial Times combined force has struck 13,000 targets since Feb 28, with approximately 3,000 remaining. White House confirmed 150 Iranian vessels destroyed, including 92% of Iran's largest warships. IDF reported 170 targets struck in the past day alone. Trump also said he wants to 'take the oil in Iran' and capture Kharg Island.
dl: MILESTONE — 13,000 targets struck; 92% of Iran's large warships destroyed

title: Houthis attack Israel for 3rd time — 2 drones intercepted near Eilat
detail: IDF intercepted two Houthi drones near Eilat, southern Israel, marking the third Houthi attack since entering the war on March 28. Houthi involvement raises concerns about renewed Red Sea shipping disruptions.
dl: INTERCEPTED — 3rd Houthi attack since entering war

title: Hezbollah: 65 attacks in 24 hours; 5,000 projectiles since March 2
detail: Hezbollah claimed 65 attacks targeting Israeli forces in 24 hours between March 29-30. Israeli FM Saar said Hezbollah has launched 5,000 missiles, rockets, and drones from Lebanon at Israel since March 2.
dl: ONGOING — 5,000 Hezbollah projectiles since March 2

title: Ukraine signs defense deals with Saudi, UAE, Qatar; offers sea drones for Hormuz
detail: Zelensky met Jordan's King Abdullah II in Amman after signing defense agreements with Saudi Arabia, UAE, and Qatar. Ukraine offered to deploy unmanned surface vessels to help clear the Strait of Hormuz, citing experience unblocking Black Sea trade routes. Zelensky also confirmed Russia providing Iran satellite imagery of US military assets.
dl: DIPLOMATIC — Ukraine offers sea drones for Hormuz; signs Gulf defense deals

title: Gulf allies privately urging Trump to continue war
detail: AP reported Saudi Arabia, UAE, Kuwait, and Bahrain officials are privately urging Trump to continue the war, arguing Tehran hasn't been weakened enough. Gulf nations want significant leadership changes or dramatic behavioral shift before any ceasefire. This despite initial complaints about lack of advance notice.
dl: DIPLOMATIC — Gulf allies press Trump to keep fighting

title: US bombs Isfahan — massive fireball
detail: Major US airstrike hit Isfahan early Tuesday, sending a massive fireball into the sky. Trump shared video of the explosion on Truth Social. Isfahan is home to sites linked to Iran's highly enriched uranium storage. Satellite imagery from June 2025 showed a truck carrying 18 containers of up to 534kg of 60%-enriched uranium entering tunnel at Isfahan Nuclear Technology Center.
dl: MASSIVE — Fireball over Isfahan; enriched uranium storage site

title: IDF warns Tehran's Vardavard area, strikes military infrastructure
detail: Israeli military posted social media warning to residents of Tehran's Vardavard area to stay home while attacking military infrastructure. Explosions heard across parts of Tehran. Continued systematic bombardment of the capital entering Day 32.
dl: STRUCK — Targeted Tehran military infrastructure with civilian warning

title: Iran launches missiles at Israel — intercepted over Jerusalem
detail: Iran launched missiles toward Israel early Tuesday. Sirens sounded and at least 10 blasts heard over Jerusalem. IDF said air defenses were working to intercept. IDF later confirmed it was safe to leave shelters.
dl: INTERCEPTED — Missiles over Jerusalem, sirens across Israel

title: Gulf-wide missile/drone attacks continue — Saudi intercepts 8 missiles
detail: Saudi Arabia intercepted 8 missiles, most targeting Riyadh. Kuwait responded to drone and missile attacks. UAE forces responded to Iranian drones and missiles; drone hit Thuraya telecoms building in Sharjah. Bahrain activated warning sirens. Interceptor debris fell on houses in southern Dubai, injuring 4 Asian nationals.
dl: ONGOING — Saudi intercepts 8 missiles; UAE, Kuwait, Bahrain all hit

title: 4 Israeli soldiers killed in southern Lebanon — total reaches 10
detail: Israeli military reported four soldiers killed and three injured during combat with Hezbollah in southern Lebanon on Tuesday. Total Israeli soldiers killed in the Lebanon ground operation now reaches 10. IDF continuing to expand 30km security zone.
dl: CASUALTIES — 4 more IDF soldiers killed; 10 total in Lebanon

title: 2 more UN peacekeepers killed in Lebanon — France calls emergency UNSC meeting
detail: Two Indonesian UN peacekeepers killed when explosion of unknown origin destroyed their vehicle in southern Lebanon. A third peacekeeper was killed Sunday. Two more wounded, one seriously. IDF opened investigation. France called for urgent UN Security Council meeting, subsequently scheduled for Tuesday.
dl: KILLED — 2 Indonesian peacekeepers dead; 3 total UN deaths; UNSC meeting called

title: China: 3 vessels transit Strait of Hormuz — Beijing expresses 'gratitude'
detail: China's foreign ministry confirmed three Chinese vessels transited the Strait of Hormuz following 'coordination with relevant parties.' Beijing expressed gratitude, signaling a separate diplomatic channel with Iran for Chinese shipping access.
dl: DIPLOMATIC — China secures Hormuz passage for 3 vessels

title: Iran parliament approves Hormuz toll plan; bans US/Israeli ships
detail: Iranian parliamentary committee approved draft law to impose tolls and duties on ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz, treating it as a standard transit corridor. Plan would also ban transit by American and Israeli vessels. Requires full Parliament approval. Rubio called plan illegal under international law.
dl: LEGISLATIVE — Parliament committee approves Hormuz toll law

title: Eurozone inflation jumps to 2.5% as energy prices surge
detail: Eurostat reported consumer prices in the 21 eurozone countries rose 2.5% annually in March, up from 1.9% in February, driven by energy prices rising 4.9% after 12 months of decline. ECB President Lagarde warned of potential rate hikes if inflation exceeds 2% target.
dl: ECONOMIC — Eurozone inflation spikes to 2.5% on war energy costs

title: War casualties at one month: 2,800+ killed across all theaters
detail: At the one-month mark: At least 1,574 Iranian civilians killed (236 children) per HRANA. Over 1,230 Lebanese killed, 3,543 wounded since March 2. At least 50 killed in Gulf nations. 17 killed in Israel. 13 US service members dead, hundreds wounded. 3 UN peacekeepers killed. Total exceeds 2,800 dead.
dl: MILESTONE — One month: 2,800+ killed across all theaters

title: Netanyahu: War goals 'beyond the halfway point'
detail: Israeli PM Netanyahu told Newsmax the war had achieved more than half its aims but declined to put a timeline on when it would end. Floated post-war plan to divert Gulf oil/gas pipelines across Saudi Arabia to Red Sea and up to Israeli Mediterranean ports. Rubio criticized NATO allies, especially Spain, for denying US overflight rights.
dl: ASSESSMENT — Netanyahu: war missions 'beyond halfway'

title: Israel completes 'wide-scale' strike wave on Tehran
detail: Massive explosions rocked Tehran at ~5:30 AM local time. IDF said it completed a wave of strikes targeting Iranian government infrastructure. State TV reported three locations hit including Nobonyad Square (military buildings/housing). Strikes also hit area near former US embassy ('Den of Spies' museum), damaging walls and nearby shops. Residents reported homes shaking from blast waves with large smoke plumes visible.
dl: HEAVY — Multiple government sites struck across Tehran

title: Tofigh Daru pharmaceutical plant destroyed in Tehran
detail: Israeli strike destroyed the Tofigh Daru pharmaceutical raw material units in Tehran, including its R&D department. Iranian officials called it 'a blow to the national medical supply chain.' IDF claimed the facility was supplying a potentially deadly drug to an Iranian chemical weapons developer. FM Araghchi denounced it as 'an attempt to disrupt the country's health care system.'
dl: DESTROYED — Pharmaceutical plant leveled; supply chain disrupted

title: Bandar Abbas — Shahid Haghani passenger port bombed
detail: Israeli fighter jets bombed the Shahid Haghani commercial and passenger port in Bandar Abbas, strategically located on the Strait of Hormuz. Deputy governor of Hormozgan Province Ahmad Nafisi condemned the 'criminal' attack on civilian infrastructure. No casualties reported.
dl: MODERATE — Strategic port infrastructure damaged

title: Qeshm Island desalination plant knocked out
detail: A desalination plant on Qeshm Island in the Strait of Hormuz was struck and knocked out of service, cutting water supply to the island. Reported by Iranian state media.
dl: SEVERE — Desalination plant destroyed; island water supply cut

title: Iranian missiles hit central Israel — 14 wounded, child critical
detail: Iran launched multiple missile salvos at central Israel throughout the morning (4+ attacks). At least 14 wounded including an 11-year-old girl in critical condition from shrapnel. Sheba Medical Center treated 8 people, 6 of them children. IDF intercepted some missiles but fragments caused damage at several sites. Air raid sirens activated across central Israel.
dl: MODERATE — Multiple salvos; 14 wounded incl. children

title: Iranian drones strike Kuwait airport fuel tanks — large fire
detail: Iranian drones struck fuel storage tanks at Kuwait International Airport, igniting a large fire. Significant damage to tanks but no casualties. This follows a previous drone strike that damaged the airport's radar system on March 29. Airport has been closed since February 28; Saudi Arabia providing buses to alternate airports.
dl: HEAVY — Fuel tanks hit; large fire at airport

title: 3 cruise missiles launched at Qatar — tanker struck
detail: Three cruise missiles launched from Iran at Qatar. Qatari military intercepted two; the third struck oil tanker 'Aqua 1' leased to QatarEnergy in Qatari territorial waters. All 21 crew evacuated safely, no casualties or environmental impact. Qatar defense ministry confirmed the attack.
dl: MODERATE — Cruise missile hits tanker in Qatari waters

title: Bahrain hit — fire at business facility from Iranian attack
detail: Air raid sirens sounded multiple times in Bahrain. Authorities confirmed an Iranian attack caused a fire at an unnamed company's facility. Residents told to remain calm and head to nearest safe places.
dl: MODERATE — Iranian attack ignites fire at business facility

title: Drone debris kills 1 in Fujairah, UAE
detail: A Bangladeshi national was killed in Fujairah, UAE after debris from an intercepted drone fell on a farm. Confirmed by Fujairah authorities.
dl: FATAL — 1 killed by falling drone debris

title: Houthis launch 3rd missile attack on Israel — joint operation with Iran & Hezbollah
detail: Yemen's Houthis claimed their third missile attack on Israel, firing a barrage of ballistic missiles at southern Israel. Spokesman Yahya Saree said it was conducted 'jointly with our mujahideen brothers in Iran and Hezbollah in Lebanon.' IDF said it intercepted a missile launched from Yemen.
dl: INTERCEPTED — 3rd Houthi-Iran-Hezbollah joint missile strike

title: Israeli strikes kill 7 in Beirut; senior Hezbollah commander targeted
detail: Israeli strikes in Beirut killed at least 7 people and wounded 24 others. IDF said it struck a senior Hezbollah commander and an additional senior operative in two separate strikes in the Beirut area. Continued strikes across southern Lebanon as Israel expands ground invasion. Lebanon's army forced to reposition units encircled by Israeli incursion.
dl: HEAVY — 7 killed; senior Hezbollah commander targeted

title: Trump: War could end in 2-3 weeks; national address tonight
detail: President Trump said the US military campaign could end in 'two to three weeks' and that a deal with Iran is not required. White House announced Trump will deliver a national address at 9 PM ET. Trump claimed he achieved the primary goal of preventing Iran's nuclear weapon, though no evidence the near-bomb-grade fuel stockpile was destroyed. Defense Sec Hegseth said US has such air superiority that B-52s are flying directly over Iranian territory.
dl: POLITICAL — Trump signals war endgame; national address tonight

title: Iran FM: No negotiations with US; 'zero trust' in Washington
detail: Iranian FM Abbas Araghchi told Al Jazeera that Tehran has neither responded to the US 15-point peace proposal nor made a counteroffer. Said messages have been exchanged with Witkoff but 'there are no grounds for negotiations' and Iran has 'zero' trust in the US. Iran says it is prepared for 'at least six months' of war.
dl: DIPLOMATIC — Iran rejects US peace plan; prepared for 6+ months

title: Oil plunges on war-end hopes; Brent drops 5% to $98.77
detail: Oil prices tumbled after Trump's comments about ending the war in weeks. Brent crude fell 5% to $98.77/barrel, WTI dropped ~4% to $97.28. European stock markets surged — Frankfurt and Paris up 2%+, London FTSE 100 up 1.7%. UAE banned Iranian nationals from entering or transiting the country.
dl: ECONOMIC — Oil drops 5% on war-end optimism; stocks rally

title: Updated death tolls — Day 33: casualties mounting across all theaters
detail: HRANA reports at least 1,598 Iranian civilians killed (244 children). Lebanon: 1,260+ killed, 3,750+ wounded since March 2. Gulf nations: 50+ killed. Israel: 17+ killed. US: 13 service members dead, hundreds wounded. IDF claims 800+ air force attack flights dropping ~16,000 munitions on Iran.
dl: MILESTONE — Day 33: Death tolls continue rising across all fronts

title: US strikes destroy B1 bridge between Tehran and Karaj
detail: US airstrikes caused partial collapse of Iran's B1 bridge, a massive highway bridge under construction connecting Tehran to Karaj. At least 13 killed and 95 wounded. Iranian state media reported a second strike hit as emergency workers arrived. Trump celebrated on social media: 'The biggest bridge in Iran comes tumbling down — Much more to follow!' Iran's FM Araghchi responded: 'Striking civilian structures will not compel Iranians to surrender.'
dl: HEAVY — Major highway bridge partially collapsed; 13 killed, 95 wounded

title: Pasteur Institute of Iran destroyed in Tehran strike
detail: Airstrike destroyed the Pasteur Institute of Iran, a renowned public health institution that produced and distributed vaccines. Iranian health ministry called it a 'direct assault on international health security.' Unclear if US or Israel responsible. Institute was one of Iran's oldest medical research facilities.
dl: DESTROYED — Major public health institution obliterated

title: US-Israeli strikes halt two major Iranian steel plants
detail: Strikes halted services at two major Iranian steel plants in Isfahan and Ahvaz, state-affiliated Mizan agency reported. Part of escalating infrastructure targeting following Trump's 'stone ages' threat. Iran's steel industry is a key economic sector.
dl: HEAVY — Two major steel plants knocked offline

satelliteImages.description: Airbus Pléiades imagery shows damage to the melting and casting hall, Direct Reduction Unit 2, and Zamzam 3 megamodule at Khuzestan Steel Company after late-March strikes
Airbus imagery confirms damage to the steelmaking unit at Mobarakeh Steel Company after the March 27 and March 31 strike rounds

title: Strikes on Qeshm Island warehouses in Persian Gulf
detail: Combined US-Israeli force struck warehouses at a port in eastern Qeshm Island on April 1-2. Satellite imagery from April 2 showed large damage at the site. Part of ongoing Strait of Hormuz campaign targeting Iranian island positions.
dl: MODERATE — Port warehouses destroyed

satelliteImages.description: Sentinel-2 imagery shows smoke rising and damage at Qeshm Island port infrastructure after U.S.-Israeli strikes on Bahman port and Doha fishing pier

title: Iranian missile strikes Israeli drone factory in Petah Tikva
detail: An Iranian ballistic missile struck an Israeli drone factory in Petah Tikva, near Tel Aviv. Building was damaged. Israel's Health Ministry reported treating 148 people in the last day, majority for minor injuries. 6,594 total treated since war began.
dl: MODERATE — Drone factory damaged by Iranian ballistic missile

title: 40-nation UK-led summit on Strait of Hormuz — no agreement reached
detail: UK Foreign Sec Yvette Cooper hosted 40+ country virtual meeting on reopening Strait of Hormuz. Traffic plunged from 150 to 10-20 vessels/day. Russia, China, France blocked UN Security Council authorization of force. Military planners to meet next week. EU's Kallas called for scaling Aspides naval mission. Russia's Kremlin aide said strait 'is open for us.' Oil surged 7.8% to $109/barrel Brent.
dl: DIPLOMATIC — 40 nations discuss Hormuz; no force agreement; oil at $109

title: 100+ law experts warn US strikes may constitute war crimes
detail: Over 100 international law experts signed open letter warning US conduct 'raises serious concerns about violations of international humanitarian law, including potential war crimes.' Cited Trump's 'just for fun' remark, Hegseth's 'stupid rules of engagement' comment, Minab school strike, and threats to hit power/water plants. War costing US $1-2 billion/day.
dl: LEGAL — 100+ experts warn of potential war crimes

title: Iran detains human rights lawyer Nasrin Sotoudeh
detail: Iranian authorities detained Nasrin Sotoudeh, prominent human rights lawyer known for defending women accused of not wearing headscarves. She has been arrested and imprisoned multiple times for her work. Detention amid wartime crackdown.
dl: INTERNAL — Prominent rights lawyer detained during wartime

title: Iran shoots down US F-15E fighter jet — first warplane downed in war
detail: Iran shot down a US F-15E Strike Eagle over Kohgiluyeh and Boyer-Ahmad province in southwestern Iran. First American warplane shot down by Iran in the five-week war. One crew member rescued, one missing — both US and Iran racing to find the missing airman. Presents major escalation and potential POW crisis if crew member captured.
dl: CRITICAL — First US warplane downed; 1 crew member missing

title: Second US aircraft (A-10) crashes near Strait of Hormuz
detail: A second US Air Force combat aircraft, an A-10 Warthog, crashed near the Strait of Hormuz. Iranian state media claimed it was hit by air defenses. The pilot was rescued. Two US aircraft losses in one day marks significant escalation in Iranian air defense capability.
dl: SIGNIFICANT — Second US aircraft down in one day; pilot rescued

title: Iranian drones hit Kuwait's largest oil refinery at Mina Al-Ahmadi
detail: Drone struck Mina Al-Ahmadi refinery, Kuwait's largest, setting several units ablaze. Kuwait Petroleum Corporation confirmed fires; no injuries reported. Kuwait Army air defense systems activated against hostile missiles and drones. Refinery had been targeted previously during the war.
dl: HEAVY — Multiple refinery units ablaze after drone strike

title: UAE Habshan gas facility hit; Saudi intercepts dozen drones
detail: Fire at UAE's Habshan gas facility caused by debris from intercepted Iranian attack. Saudi Arabia intercepted and destroyed about a dozen Iranian drones. Iranian missiles also targeted Israel overnight, with continued rocket volleys from Hezbollah.
dl: MODERATE — Gas facility damaged; Saudi intercepts drones

title: Pentagon ousts Army Chief of Staff Gen. Randy George
detail: Defense Sec Hegseth forced Army Chief of Staff Gen. Randy George to retire immediately, despite having 1+ year left on his term. George had served since 2023. Part of ongoing clash between Hegseth and senior Army officers. Hegseth has fired 12+ senior military officers since taking over Pentagon.
dl: POLITICAL — Army Chief of Staff forced out amid wartime upheaval

title: Oil surges to $109 as war intensifies; Hormuz traffic at 10-20 ships/day
detail: Brent crude jumped ~8% to $109/barrel on Friday after two US planes downed and Gulf refinery attacks. War cost estimated at $1-2 billion/day for US. Strait of Hormuz traffic plunged from 150 to 10-20 vessels/day. 40-nation talks yielded no force agreement. Macron called military reopening 'unrealistic.'
dl: ECONOMIC — Oil surges to $109; Hormuz nearly closed; war costs $1-2B/day

title: Updated death tolls — Day 35: 1,606 Iranian civilians, 1,345 Lebanese killed
detail: HRANA: 1,606 Iranian civilians killed including 244 children. Lebanon health ministry: 1,345 killed, 4,040 wounded since latest fighting. Gulf nations: 50+ killed. Israel: 17+ killed, 6,594 treated. US: 13 service members dead + 1 missing (F-15E crew). Bellingcat reports UAE downplaying Iranian attack damage.
dl: MILESTONE — Day 35: Rising tolls; US airman missing; 2 aircraft lost

title: US rescues downed F-15E airman from southwestern Iran
detail: U.S. forces extracted the final unrecovered crew member from the F-15E shot down on April 3, ending a high-profile search-and-rescue operation behind enemy lines in southwestern Iran after a two-day race with Iranian forces.
dl: STRATEGIC — US recovers missing crew member after downing of first warplane

title: Mahshahr petrochemical zone struck
